free_text: Thin-target cross sections for some Cr,Mn,Co,Ni and Zn
nuclides produced in copper by 82 to 416 MeV protons
The University of Chicago
Synchrocyclotron.
Three series of bombardments were performed on thin
29.8 mg/cm**2 thickness, pure (>99.99%) copper metal
foils.
Empirical formulas and Monte Carlo treatments.
Any information is absent.
The start of chemical separation after end of
irradiation is unknown.
Irradiation duration is unknown.
No information about source of uncertainty
A standard deviation -1 sigma- of +-20% is assigned
to each individual cross-section value. Such
uncertainties are larger than the statistical
uncertainties of the radioactivity measurements,
typically appr. 1%, or the variation observed in
sequential measurements on an individual - decaying-
sample, typically appr. 5%. However, these quoted
uncertainties of +-20% reflect typical agreement among
values obtained from replicate experiments, and an
assessment of the uncertainties in the values for other
parameters used in calculating cross sections from
experimental data.
Tbl.I from Phys.Rev.,C6(1972)1235
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
SD: Corrections in several Subents.
ERR-T -> DATA-ERR. MONITOR updated.
/by authors/ Standard samples prepared from national
bureau of standards calibrated solutions were used
in detection efficiency determinations for the nuclides
so indicated.
SD: SF5=IND deleted from REACTION code.
/by Authors/.Only Relative Values have Been Reported
For Ni-63 Because of Insufficient Knowledge of the
Absolute Detection Efficiency For the Low-Energy
Radiation (0.067-MEV B-) Sought.
SD: SF4=Co-60g -> Co-60; SF5=IND/M+ deleted
from REACTION code.
Independent.

section was subtracted.
- By Compiler - It is Unknown if the Ni-56 cross
section was subtracted.
